rules.title: A flag mechanism exists
rules.lesson: release flags decouple deployment from release — a misbehaving feature becomes disableable without redeploying, shortening the recovery path. Honesty: no source quantifies kill-switch → MTTR (inference joining two sourced ends)
rules.rationale: The defining purpose of release flags is decoupling deployment from release — a misbehaving feature becomes disableable without redeploying, which shortens the recovery path. No primary source quantifies kill-switch → MTTR; the rationale claims the shorter path, not a measured number. Heuristic SDK-import scan.
rules.fix: Adopt a flag mechanism (OpenFeature SDK + provider, or a declared config-driven store) and register flags per FLAG-02.

rules.title: Every flag has a registry record with an owner
rules.lesson: one flag, one record (records/flags/FLAG-NNNN.json); an unnamed-owner flag is how "who can turn this off?" becomes a 3am archaeology dig. Empty active registry WARNs, never a vacuous pass
rules.rationale: One flag, one record (records/flags/FLAG-NNNN.json). The canonical article names exactly this metadata set; an unnamed-owner flag is how "who can turn this off?" becomes a 3am archaeology dig. When the family is active but zero records exist, the rule WARNs (an empty registry is a finding, not a vacuous pass).
rules.fix: cp templates/flag.json records/flags/FLAG-NNNN.json per live flag; fill name, owner, type, created, review_by.

rules.title: Flags carry expiry; none past-due
rules.lesson: flags are inventory with a carrying cost; stale flags are an operational hazard. Mechanical "time bomb". Permanent types (kill-switch, permission) carry long review horizons instead
rules.rationale: Flags are inventory with a carrying cost; stale flags are an operational hazard, verbatim from the sources. This is the mechanical version of the cited "time bomb" practice. Permanent types (kill-switch, permission) need no near-term expiry; they carry long review horizons instead. Deterministic date math against the run date.
rules.fix: Set/repair review_by per flag; retire past-due flags or record pipeline jdg new --kind risk-acceptance --subject FLAG-03 --reason "flag <name> kept because ..." --review-by <date> with a tripwire.
